html:
The proposal suggests a new law regarding international sanctions. The proposed changes implement the EU's sanctions directive into Swedish law.

The main proposals are:

  • To increase the penalties for violations of international sanctions.
  • To criminalize minor violations of international sanctions.
  • To introduce new criminal offenses, including "sanctions violation," "sanctions offense," "aggravated sanctions offense," and "repeated sanctions offense."
  • To criminalize attempts and aiding and abetting certain sanctions offenses.
  • To introduce a reporting obligation, requiring certain authorities to report to the police or the prosecuting authority if there is reason to believe that offenses under the new law have been committed.

The new law and other legal amendments are proposed to come into effect on May 20, 2025.

Proposed Parliamentary Decisions

The government's proposals:

  1. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for a law on international sanctions.
  2. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Swedish Criminal Code.
  3. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Aliens Act (2005:716).
  4. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the law (2023:677) on the freezing of assets.
  5. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Customs Powers Act (2024:710).
tldr: Sweden is proposing a new law to comply with EU regulations regarding international sanctions. This will result in stricter penalties for those who violate these regulations.
lattlast:
The government has proposed a new law regarding international sanctions. The purpose is to ensure that Sweden complies with the regulations within the EU. Violations of these regulations will result in stricter penalties than currently in place.

The proposal introduces new types of offenses, such as sanctions violations. It will also become illegal to attempt to violate the regulations or to assist someone else in doing so. Even minor breaches of the rules will be considered offenses that may lead to penalties.

A key element of the proposal is that certain authorities must report to the Police Authority or the Public Prosecution Office if they suspect that someone has violated the new regulations. This is known as a reporting obligation, meaning that the authorities have a duty to report suspected offenses.

The proposal also includes amendments to several other laws, such as the Aliens Act and regulations for customs. If the Parliament votes in favor of the proposal, the new regulations are planned to come into effect on May 20, 2025.
